_meta.author: Claude (scorecard-refine-local autopilot)
_meta.note: Fort Lauderdale, FL — LOCAL evidence finalization for 4 sitting commissioners + Mayor Trantalis (evidence_local) + Warren Sturman status fix (lost 2024-11-05 to Ben Sorensen, now FORMER). All TRUE/FALSE cells cite primary or reputable secondary sources. Pride-crosswalk-defense votes (Aug 2025) score the rubric's literal refuse_state_overreach cell TRUE for all sitting commissioners with deviation note (resistance asserts local control / subsidiarity, motive is LGBT-symbol defense which is anti-rubric in substance — TRUE on the action, flagged in notes). Christian-liberty cells left null where the only act is routine ceremonial; high bar enforced.

profile.religion: Greek Orthodox Christian (St. Demetrios Greek Orthodox Church, Fort Lauderdale — 40+ year communicant)
profile.background: 42nd Mayor of Fort Lauderdale (since March 2018); reelected Nov 2024 to a third and final 4-year term (through 2028). Stetson JD; real-estate / civil-rights attorney. First openly gay mayor of a major US city; Victory Fund and Equality Florida endorsed.

dean-trantalis.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE LOCAL refinement] Roster verified: Mayor, Fort Lauderdale, third and final term to 2028 (Wikipedia 2024 race; Hoodline 2024-11). HARD evidence: spoke at Broward Democratic Party abortion-rights rally May 7, 2022 (life FALSE); personally delivered June 2024 Pride Month proclamation, introduced Progressive Pride street installation, denounced rainbow-flag vandalism (marriage FALSE); city held flat 4.1193 millage 19 consecutive years and Trantalis publicly opposed a proposed EMS tax (economic TRUE — fiscal restraint); affirmatively expanded FLPD by 35 officers across two budgets and publicly backed chief leadership stability (public_justice TRUE — deviation flag for D mayor); Aug 27, 2025 led unanimous commission vote to administratively appeal FDOT order removing Pride crosswalks, then sought 4th DCA review (refuse_state_overreach TRUE — DEVIATION DIRECTION FLAG: action asserts local control / subsidiarity but motive is Pride-symbol defense, opposite the rubric's substantive frame). NO EVIDENCE found on family/christian_liberty/election/border/self_defense at the mayoral level — null. A 2021 Calvary Chapel anniversary proclamation drew LGBT-press criticism but is routine ceremonial recognition, not affirmative religious-liberty advocacy → null per high-bar discipline.

0.note: Spoke at Broward Democratic Party abortion-rights rally in downtown Fort Lauderdale opposing Dobbs reversal (2022-05-07), alongside Nikki Fried and Wasserman Schultz — public pro-abortion-access advocacy.
Personally delivered June 2024 Pride Month proclamation; introduced the Progressive Pride Street installation and Sebastian Street rainbow crosswalk; publicly decried Pride-flag vandalism.

2.note: City held flat 4.1193 millage 19 consecutive years (only Broward city to do so); Trantalis publicly opposed proposed EMS tax citing burden on property owners 'in the current economic climate.'

0.note: Affirmatively expanded FLPD by 35 additional sworn officers across two budgets; publicly backed FLPD veteran chief William Schultz; pro-police posture in protester-shoving controversy ('zero tolerance for improper behavior'). DEVIATION FLAG: D mayor of major FL city actively growing sworn headcount.

Aug 27, 2025: 'Tonight, we must stand our ground. We cannot allow us to be bullied into submission and to allow others to dictate what we should do in our own communities.' Led unanimous vote to administratively appeal FDOT crosswalk-removal order; later filed 4th DCA notice of appeal. DEVIATION DIRECTION: action is local-control / subsidiarity (rubric-aligned action) but motive is Pride-crosswalk defense (anti-rubric in substance) — scored TRUE on the action per skill discipline, flagged in notes.

profile.background: Fort Lauderdale City Commissioner District 1 (sworn Dec 2022). Previously Fort Lauderdale City Auditor (~2006-2022) — established and led the office for ~16 years until the Commission fired him Feb 2022 over an audit of Police Chief Larry Scirotto's off-duty NCAA referee hours; he then flipped the D1 seat in Nov 2022 against the body that fired him. Announced resignation May 1, 2026 effective Nov 2, 2026; withdrawing 2028 re-election candidacy; special election Nov 3, 2026 to fill the remainder of the term. Reelected to a new 4-year term Nov 2024.

john-herbst.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE LOCAL refinement] Roster verified: D1 Commissioner sworn Dec 2022; reelected Nov 2024; ANNOUNCED RESIGNATION May 1, 2026 effective 11:59pm Nov 2, 2026 — special election Nov 3, 2026 (WLRN 2026-05-01; Florida Politics 2026). Status currently active; will become FORMER Nov 3, 2026. PROMPT/DATA CORRECTION: prior bio implying 'Broward County Clerk auditor' is incorrect — he was Fort Lauderdale City Auditor (Local10 2022-02-19). PARTY: nonpartisan office; existing party tag 'R' not directly confirmed in surfaced sources — preserved as recorded but flagged for verification. HARD evidence: sole 'no' vote on the City Hall Partners P3 deal; vocal critic of the Inter Miami / Lockhart Park stadium arrangement ('worst deal in the world, business plan deeply flawed') (economic TRUE — fiscal-dissenter posture); personally issued May 12, 2024 National Police Week proclamation to FOP Lodge 31, paying tribute to 13 fallen FLPD officers; police + fire union endorsements (public_justice TRUE — affirmative beyond routine budget). Aug 2025 voted with unanimous commission to administratively appeal FDOT Pride-crosswalk removal order (refuse_state_overreach TRUE — same DEVIATION DIRECTION flag as Trantalis). DEVIATION: Herbst also supported establishing an EMS taxing district ('It's either a millage rate increase or an EMS tax'), undercutting a pure tax-hawk read. NO EVIDENCE on life/marriage/family/christian_liberty/election/border/self_defense individually — null. The 4-1 vote on a separate legal-remedies motion (CBS Miami 2025-08-28) does not attribute the lone dissent to Herbst, so marriage q0 is null rather than soft-FALSE.

2.note: Sole 'no' vote on the City Hall Partners FTL P3 deal; sustained vocal opposition to the Inter Miami / Lockhart Park stadium arrangement as a 'deeply flawed business plan'; ~16-yr City Auditor record establishes fiscal-dissenter posture. Caveat: also supported establishing a new EMS taxing district (EMS1 coverage).

0.note: Personally issued and presented May 12, 2024 National Police Week proclamation to FOP Lodge 31, naming 13 fallen FLPD officers — affirmative pro-police advocacy (ceremonial but specific), plus documented police/fire union endorsements.

Voted in the unanimous Aug 27, 2025 commission action to administratively appeal FDOT order removing Pride crosswalks — local-control assertion. DEVIATION DIRECTION: substantive issue (Pride-symbol defense) is anti-rubric; scored TRUE on the local-control action per skill discipline.

profile.religion: Christian — ordained minister with doctorate in theology (Midwest Theological University); daughter of Rev. James E. Beasley Sr. Specific congregation not surfaced in public sources.
profile.background: First Black woman elected to Fort Lauderdale City Commission (District 3, sworn Dec 6, 2022). Native of Fort Lauderdale; 32-year career at Broward Sheriff's Office, retiring as Extraditions Specialist (started as 911 Communications Operator); president of Historic Dorsey-Riverbend Civic Association; founder/CEO of Unified Central Broward Neighborhood Alliance.

pamela-beasley-pittman.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE LOCAL refinement] Roster verified via city website (fortlauderdale.gov/.../commissioner-pamela-beasley-pittman). 2026 re-election outcome NOT directly surfaced in this pass; presence in Aug 27, 2025 commission action confirms tenure through at least mid-2025 — recommend status re-check post-March-2026 cycle. HARD evidence: Aug 27, 2025 voted to appeal FDOT order removing Pride crosswalks with on-record quote 'We should fight this out to the end' (marriage FALSE; refuse_state_overreach TRUE with same direction-deviation flag as Trantalis); 32-yr BSO career plus on-record Sistrunk-corridor pro-police-partnership statement ('We are working with our law enforcement community, neighbors coming together where we are mentoring our young people') clears the affirmative-advocacy high bar (public_justice TRUE — career + statement, not budget vote). Heavy personal-faith vocabulary ('this assignment is ordained by God,' 'standing on my faith') but NO documented commission action on invocations, religious displays, or church partnerships — christian_liberty null per high-bar discipline. NO EVIDENCE on life/family/economic/election/border/self_defense individually — null. EXPECTED DEVIATION (Black-church D pro-life or traditional-marriage stance) was NOT documented; marriage cell scored from her own Pride-crosswalk vote, not party prior.

0.note: Aug 27, 2025: voted to appeal FDOT removal order on Pride crosswalks with on-record quote 'We should fight this out to the end' — affirmative pro-LGBTQ-symbolism action.

32-year BSO career (retired Extraditions Specialist) plus on-record Sistrunk-corridor pro-police-partnership statement: 'We are working with our law enforcement community, neighbors coming together where we are mentoring our young people' — affirmative advocacy + biography, not just budget vote.

Voted with commission to appeal FDOT removal order asserting local control. DEVIATION DIRECTION: substantive issue (Pride-symbol defense) is anti-rubric; TRUE on the local-control action per skill discipline.

profile.background: City Commissioner District 2, City of Fort Lauderdale (sworn March 20, 2018); reelected Nov 2020 and Nov 2024 to a third and final 4-year term (through ~2029). Born NYC 1953; BA + EdM SUNY Buffalo; 18-yr educator. Worked as Arts Education / Community Development Program Manager at the Broward County Cultural Division. Openly gay; Victory Fund and Dolphin Democrats endorsed.

steven-glassman.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE LOCAL refinement] Roster verified: D2 Commissioner, third and final term Nov 2024-2029 (fortlauderdale.gov; OutSFL 2024-11-07). HARD evidence: personally presented June 2024 Pride Month proclamation ('The city proudly reaffirms its commitment to protecting and respecting the rights of every resident… by flying the rainbow flag above city hall during this month of June for the fourth year in a row') and led 2025 'last man standing' defense of rainbow/Pulse-memorial crosswalks against FDOT removal order (marriage FALSE; refuse_state_overreach TRUE — DEVIATION DIRECTION: 'For me, it's really about home rule. Do we have control over our own streets?'); March 16, 2021 publicly attacked a city proclamation honoring Coral Ridge Presbyterian Church (D. James Kennedy's congregation) as 'sanitized and whitewashed history of Dr. Kennedy and the church' — affirmative public statement against recognition of an evangelical institution (christian_liberty FALSE — rare documented direct anti-evangelical-church statement from a sitting D commissioner); publicly thanked Budget Advisory Board for budget HOLDING the millage rate flat at 4.1193 for 16th consecutive year (economic TRUE — narrow); publicly backed new $100M voter-approved FLPD HQ ('the new police headquarters will make a strong statement to the community that the City of Fort Lauderdale is committed to law enforcement') (public_justice TRUE). 2023-12-19 homelessness vote — proposed deducting Food Not Bombs legal-fees judgment from next year's homeless-services budget, voted down 5-0 (deviation note: harder-edged on homelessness than his own mayor). NO EVIDENCE on life/family/election/border/self_defense — null.

0.note: Personally presented June Pride Month proclamation reaffirming city's flying of the rainbow flag over city hall a 4th consecutive year; led 2025 'last man standing' defense of rainbow/Pulse-memorial crosswalks.

March 16, 2021 commission meeting: read prepared statement publicly attacking a city proclamation honoring Coral Ridge Presbyterian Church (D. James Kennedy's congregation) as 'sanitized and whitewashed history of Dr. Kennedy and the church' — affirmative public opposition to recognition of an evangelical institution.

2.note: Publicly thanked Budget Advisory Board for FY budget holding the millage rate flat at 4.1193 for the 16th consecutive year.

0.note: Publicly backed new $100M voter-approved FLPD HQ: 'the new police headquarters will make a strong statement to the community that the City of Fort Lauderdale is committed to law enforcement.'

'For me, it's really about home rule. Do we have control over our own streets? Are we able to express ourselves, whether it's street art or anything else?' 'We are the last man standing.' DEVIATION DIRECTION: local-control assertion (rubric-aligned action) in service of Pride-symbol defense (anti-rubric in substance) — TRUE on action.

profile.background: Former City Commissioner District 4, City of Fort Lauderdale (Dec 2022 – Dec 3, 2024). Internist (Internal Medicine ~40 yrs); University of Miami Miller School of Medicine 1984; hospital affiliations Broward Health Medical Center, Holy Cross Health, Mount Sinai. Lifelong Broward County resident; BSEE Honors University of Florida.

warren-sturman.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE LOCAL refinement — STATUS FIX] Sturman is NO LONGER the D4 Commissioner. Lost the Nov 5, 2024 general election to Ben Sorensen, finishing 3rd behind Kevin Cochrane. Sorensen was sworn in Dec 3, 2024 and currently serves as Vice Mayor / D4. Term as commissioner: Dec 2022 – Dec 3, 2024 (single term, originally won by 49 votes in a 2022 special election filling the seat Sorensen vacated for a congressional run). Cells left as previously recorded (reset_unspecified: false for this record — status fix only, not a re-score). FOLLOWUP: add/verify ben-sorensen as the active D4 record. PROMPT/DATA CORRECTION: prior bio implying Cleveland Clinic affiliation is incorrect — actual affiliations are Broward Health Medical Center, Holy Cross Health, and Mount Sinai.
